video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Diego with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Nola on Fifth
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm trying to take you to New Orleans. Well, at least to a place that's serving up real-deal New Orleans food. I hear there's a chef drove all the way out here, and he's doing it right. Let's check it out. This is Nola on Fifth. Feels like New Orleans came to San Diego, which actually happened by way of Andrew Boyer, a cook from the Big Easy, who came here 27 years ago to help a friend open some breweries.

dishes.description: A combination poor boy featuring boiled top round beef with demi-glace and fried shrimp, served on Leidenheimer bread with remoulade sauce, lettuce, tomatoes, and house-made pickles.
dishes.guy_response: One minute you're getting crunchy shrimp and remoulade, and then you transition right into this delicious, warm shredded beef with the gravy. Rich in flavor. Leidenheimer makes all the difference in the world. Pickled veg is a must. A little unconventional. This is your house. Like my good buddy Steve Hutchinson said, I learned some stuff I knew, and I learned some stuff I didn't know. Delicious, man. Not a thing I would change. Ready to get down on the poor boys. The beef is super tender. It's unreal.

restaurants.name: Shawarma Guys
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in South Park, which is about two miles from downtown San Diego. And with a lot of hard work and determination, and no culinary training, he was able to open up his food truck. Well, he got some rating. Let's see, what was that rating again? Oh yeah, that's right. Best restaurant in America by Yelp. This is the shawarma guys. Ever since Brian Zito canceled his cell phone career plan to become a shawarma man.

dishes.description: Chicken leg and breast marinated in Greek yogurt and spices, cooked on a vertical spit, then served in a tortilla wrap with garlic paste, fattoush salad, and sumac dressing, pressed in a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: That's a culinary rainbow of Flavortown, folks. I want wallpaper made out of that. Love the radishes in there. The little added extra crunch, the bell peppers, the cucumbers, delicious. Chicken shawarma going on the fattoush wrap. The chicken is so tender. It's like the perfect blend of juicy and the crunch. I love it. Definitely one of my favorites.

restaurants.name: Tioli's Crazy Burger
restaurants.owner_chef: Lothar Manns and Wolfgang Peter Schlick
restaurants.guy_intro: When you think about a burger joint, well, you're probably thinking the All-American. The All-American burger with the shake and the fries and the whole giddy-up, unless you're here in San Diego, where two dudes from Germany who got out of fine dining, well, these cats will make a burger out of just about anything. You've got to check out Tioli's Crazy Burger, from Chef Lothar Manns and his buddy, Wolfgang Peter Schlick.

dishes.description: Sirloin cut alligator meat, seasoned and grilled, served with a unique curry fruit gravy made with dried bananas, apples, apricots, plums, cream, and curry.
dishes.guy_response: That's great. I gotta tell you, I never thought I would really like it. Look at that. That's awesome. Great flavor to it. And the curry fruit thing, you know what? That's crazy. Actually tastes like a pork cutlet. It seems like all the flavors meld together to make one very fine burger.

dishes.description: Lean kangaroo meat from Australia, grilled and seasoned, served with a horseradish cream sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Right now on Triple D, someone's enjoying the burger a little bit more than the host. Princess Bite. Great flavor, not overcooked. Horseradish cream sauce is dynamite on there. I like that. And so do you. Huh? Of course I like what I cook. Oh, I'm sorry. I'm just getting out of control. It's very lean. It's very good. It's very unique. If I didn't know it was something different, I would think it's hamburger.

restaurants.name: Grand Ole Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: All right, so I'm about 45 minutes outside of San Diego, California, hanging out with Troy Johnson, one of my favorite Triple G judges. Now, Troy, where are you bringing me? This is Grand Ole Barbecue. It has good southern barbecue, which you don't get in San Diego often. It's Central Texas barbecue, to be exact. And it's in owner and San Diego boy Andy Harris' blood, eventually teaming up with Chef Ami Cisneros on a new kind of jam.

dishes.description: Beef rib seasoned with butcher grind pepper and salt, smoked over white oak, served on buttery polenta, topped with chorizo queso and a pickled onion relish with corn chips.
dishes.guy_response: Great smoke, nice bark. Boy, that sounds really delicious. Each bite gets better. Really. What I did is I first just started with the barbecue. Now I'm enjoying some of the queso and the relish. It's delicious. I like just, mm. It's fantastic. The fresh relief that you're getting from this corn chip salsa is dynamite. All right, let's get in there. This elevates the barbecue to another level. I've done a lot of barbecue joints. I've never seen anything like this. And that relish is totally San Diego, too. That Mexican flavor, those serranos in there, that's legit.

dishes.description: Braised short ribs, seared and then slow-cooked in a rich liquid with vegetables, red wine, and beef stock, finished with caramelized onions, served on a toasted baguette with melted Gruyere cheese and a side of jus.
dishes.guy_response: The only problem with this is you didn't serve it in a kiddie pool. Because if it could get in with the sandwich and the jus, I mean, that short rib, along with those caramelized onions, bananas. And? Bananas is good. Bananas is good. The bread's fantastic. The jus is luxurious. You get all that nice fat that's coming from the braising of the short rib. Little touch of the red wine and the melted Gruyere on top. It's all the components of what you want a great French dip to be. That's it, baby.

restaurants.name: OB Noodle House
restaurants.guy_intro: All right, ho, Dad, this is the joint. Food's amazing, and the story's even better. That's my kind of joint. This is the OB Noodle House. Ocean Beach is a really small beach community. They're very proud of their identity. This restaurant definitely fits in. 40-plus taps of really good beer. And what goes with beer? Of course, wings. Spicy garlic wings. The absolute best wings that I've ever had.

dishes.description: Mid-thigh chicken wings seasoned, marinated for a day in a chili oil sauté with various spices, peanut butter, and hoisin sauce, then deep-fried and wok-sautéed with fresh garlic and seasonings, garnished with cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, my gosh. Yeah. A little bit of heat, not too much. Good amount of spice to it, a little bit of sweet. Just a little bit of the crispiness on the skin. They're tender. They're juicy. There's a lot of flavor, a lot of depth of flavor. I'm impressed. Tell me that wing is not the bomb. That's excellent. Not too spicy on the outside, but just the right amount of heat. Really, really good wing.
